title: IDs and classes
description: Naming elements so CSS and JS can find them.

questions.prompt: How many elements on a page can share the same ID?
questions.explanation: An id must be unique in the document. Use class for groups.

questions.prompt: Which is correct if the element should have BOTH classes 'btn' and 'primary'?
questions.code:
A) <button class="btn primary">
B) <button class="btn" class="primary">
C) <button class=[btn, primary]>
D) <button class="btn.primary">
questions.explanation: Multiple classes are space-separated in a single class attribute.

questions.prompt: Give this heading id="page-title" and class "headline big".
questions.explanation: IDs are unique; classes are space-separated.

questions.prompt: Which is BEST for an element that appears many times (menu items)?
questions.explanation: class is reusable — style all menu items with one rule.

questions.prompt: Two elements with the same id — what happens?
options: Undefined behavior: querySelector returns just the first, but validators complain
questions.explanation: It renders, but violates the spec. Fragment links (#id) become ambiguous.

questions.prompt: Which JS API returns an ELEMENT by id?
questions.explanation: getElementById — one of the oldest DOM methods.

questions.prompt: How do you select by class in CSS?
questions.code: /* CSS: pick the correct selector */ ??? { color: red; }
questions.explanation: Dot for class, hash for id.

questions.prompt: Valid id names must NOT contain:
questions.explanation: IDs can't contain spaces. Otherwise, most characters are allowed.
